sections.paragraph:
τολμηρός, ά, όν (τόλμη; Eur., Thu. et al.; Sir 8:15; 19:3; JosAs 23:7 cod. A 17, 7 [p. 67, 5 Bat.]; Jos., Ant. 1, 113; 14, 165; Just.) bold, daring, audacious, adv. τολμηρῶς (Thu. 3, 74, 1; 83, 3; X. et al.; Mitt-Wilck. I/2, 461, 25 [III a.d.]). Comp. τολμηρότερος (Isocr. 14, 8 Bl. and oft.; Sir 19:2; Philo, Op. M. 170): its adverbs, τολμηροτέρως Ro 15:15 v.l. and τολμηρότερον (Thu. 4, 126, 4; Polyb. 1, 17, 7) Ro 15:15, both=rather boldly.—DELG s.v. τόλμη. TW.

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

Bat. Bat. = PBatiffol, s. JosAs—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Mitt-Wilck. Mitt-Wilck. = LMitteis/UWilcken, Grundzüge und Chrestomathie der Papyruskunde—Lists 4, 6

Comp Comp date uncertain—List 5

Isocr Isocr , IV b.c.—List 5

oft. oft. = often

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1010
